text: Running velocity at VO2max is the strongest predictor of 8-km race performance among trained male runners.
Velocity at maximal lactate steady state (V-MLSS) strongly predicts 8-km race performance and is not significantly different from actual race pace.
specific_value: r = 0.92; V-MLSS 15.7 vs race pace 16.0 km/h

text: Velocity at lactate threshold (V-Tlac) provides the best estimate of maximal lactate steady state velocity among submaximal lactate measures.
Velocity at lactate threshold is not significantly different from velocity at maximal lactate steady state, making it a practical proxy for MLSS determination.
The lactate minimum test underestimates maximal lactate steady state velocity and provides a poorer estimate of V-MLSS compared to lactate threshold.
specific_value: r = 0.61, SEE = 0.75 km/h; Lac-min 14.9 vs V-MLSS 15.7 km/h (P < 0.05)

text: 8-km race pace in trained runners approximates maximal lactate steady state velocity, suggesting MLSS intensity is sustainable for roughly 25-30 minutes of racing.
specific_value: race pace 16.0 km/h vs V-MLSS 15.7 km/h
